question.text: Futures contracts define all of the following EXCEPT:

answer.text: The credit rating of the buyer and seller
The quantity and quality of the commodity
The permissible price fluctuations (minimum and maximum daily price changes)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Futures contracts specify quantity, quality, delivery date, place, and permissible price fluctuations. The credit rating of participants is NOT part of the contract specification — the clearing corporation handles credit risk. **The Trap:** All other items are explicitly standardized in commodity futures contracts. Participant identity and creditworthiness are irrelevant because the CCP guarantees settlement. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers equity futures contract specifications. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This anonymity is a strength of exchange-traded contracts — whether a small retail trader or a large institution, everyone trades on the same terms.
